evidence_text: Firetiger's ingest system for most customers was degraded for about 8 hours on March 1, 2026 and failed to accept OpenTelemetry data and GitHub webhooks.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "For approximately 8 hours on Sunday, March 1, 2026, Firetiger's ingest system for most customers was in a degraded state, failing to accept OpenTelemetry data and GitHub webhooks."
note: The source states duration and affected ingest paths, but does not quantify customer count or event loss.
caveat: The source states duration and affected ingest paths, but does not quantify customer count or event loss.

evidence_text: A CI race condition with overly aggressive concurrency control caused a build job to be erroneously canceled during PR merge on February 27, 2026.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "A race condition in our CI systems, with excessively aggressive concurrency control, resulted in a build job getting erroneously canceled during merge of a PR on February 27, 2026."
note: This evidence identifies an upstream initiating failure in the deployment chain.
caveat: This evidence identifies an upstream initiating failure in the deployment chain.

evidence_text: A second CI attribution error made a later deploy job treat the canceled build as complete and update AWS Lambda and ECS service definitions to a non-existent container ID.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "A later deploy job falsely believed the build had completed because of a second issue in our CI system's attribution of build artifacts, and proceeded to roll out the change by updating AWS Lambda and ECS service definitions to point to a non-existent container ID."
note: This evidence describes the second failure needed for the invalid rollout to proceed.
caveat: This evidence describes the second failure needed for the invalid rollout to proceed.

evidence_text: Terraform rejected the Lambda change as invalid and aborted the deployment, but ECS service definitions had already been mutated.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "This rollout was done with Terraform, which rejected the change to Lambda as invalid, aborting the deployment, but it had already mutated the ECS service definitions."
note: The deployment both failed and partially changed infrastructure, leaving the system in an inconsistent state rather than a clean rollback.
caveat: The deployment both failed and partially changed infrastructure, leaving the system in an inconsistent state rather than a clean rollback.

evidence_text: After ECS definitions became invalid, new tasks could not be put into production and any exited task could not be restarted.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "ECS's definitions were thus unable to put new tasks into production, and so traffic was not shifted, but the system was in a delicate state: if any ECS tasks exited, they would not be able to be restarted because their definition was invalid."
note: This evidence states the operational condition that turned the bad deployment into a latent failure.
caveat: This evidence states the operational condition that turned the bad deployment into a latent failure.

evidence_text: Around 5:48 UTC on March 1, restarted ECS tasks failed to come up and frontend ingest load balancers began returning 503s for all ingest events.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "About 25 hours later, around 5:48 UTC on March 1, the ECS tasks gradually did begin to get restarted, but new tasks couldn't come up, so our frontend ingest load balancers began to respond with 503s to all ingest events, including webhooks and OpenTelemetry data."
note: The source ties the visible outage onset to task restarts roughly 25 hours after the faulty rollout state was created.
caveat: The source ties the visible outage onset to task restarts roughly 25 hours after the faulty rollout state was created.

evidence_text: Firetiger's separate self-monitoring deployment detected the issue at 6:00, 12 minutes after the problem materialized.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "We use Firetiger (in a separate deployment) to monitor itself, so the system detected this issue at 6:00, 12 minutes after the problem materialized."
note: The source gives both the detection time and the 12-minute delay from incident materialization.
caveat: The source gives both the detection time and the 12-minute delay from incident materialization.

evidence_text: A detection agent flagged the abnormality but initially believed the problem was isolated to GitHub webhook ingest and only attempted to notify human operators.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "This agent detected and flagged the problem, believing it to be isolated to GitHub webhook ingest, and attempted (more on that) to notify human operators."
note: The source describes partial problem recognition and attempted escalation, which matters because the later narrative shows notification did not reach normal incident channels.
caveat: The source describes partial problem recognition and attempted escalation, which matters because the later narrative shows notification did not reach normal incident channels.

evidence_text: At 7:47, Firetiger's issue triage system coalesced multiple ingestion detections and traffic-drop findings into one issue and identified the bad ECR version as the root cause.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "At 7:47, the problem got worse, as more ingest tasks restarted. Our issue triaging subsystem... identified that multiple detection agents had found problems related to ingestion... and also identified that many other agents had found a major drop in traffic. These all got coalesced into one issue, which was easily able to dig in and find the root cause, identifying the bad version in ECR."
note: This evidence ties worsening symptoms to successful automated root-cause identification.
caveat: This evidence ties worsening symptoms to successful automated root-cause identification.

evidence_text: Human operators were notified about 8 hours late because an internal notification-system misconfiguration hid alerts from normal incident management channels.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt:
"Despite the good performance of our agents in finding the issue, it still took us about 8 hours to be notified of the problem. This was due to a second issue in our system."
"Inadvertently, they left this in a state that hid notifications from our normal incident management channels, which resulted in the long period in which we didn't detect the issue."
note: The source presents a tension between fast system detection and delayed human awareness due to notification routing failure.
caveat: The source presents a tension between fast system detection and delayed human awareness due to notification routing failure.

evidence_text: The notification misconfiguration was introduced late Friday while an engineer repeatedly deleted and recreated a policy document during testing of a deployment-attribution feature.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt:
"Late on Friday night, one of our engineers was working on our internal Firetiger deployment's issue notification system."
"While working on testing a new feature for users... they repeatedly had to delete and remake a policy document used to control where notifications could get sent."
note: The source identifies the operational context in which the notification-channel failure was created.
caveat: The source identifies the operational context in which the notification-channel failure was created.

evidence_text: After the issue report surfaced, Firetiger used Claude Code with MCP, AWS access, and GitHub CLI access to locate the broken deployment, rebuild the image, rerun deploy scripts, and restore service.
citation: Nelson, "Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents" (2026)
source_excerpt: "For approximately 8 hours on Sunday, March 1, 2026, Firetiger's ingest system for most customers was in a degraded state, failing to accept OpenTelemetry data and GitHub webhooks."
note: The source states duration and affected ingest paths, but does not quantify customer count or event loss.
caveat: The source states duration and affected ingest paths, but does not quantify customer count or event loss.
